plan.goal: Ship the v2 sync client (sofar-cloud Phase 5.1, the D14 seam): sofar login via RFC-8628 device flow, sofar link, push/pull sync with offline queue + doorbell SSE against api.sofar.sh, importable @alignlabs/sofar/client subpath — release as 0.9.0

tasks.title: Config + stores: api_url precedence (flag > SOFAR_API_URL > .sofar/remote.json > default), credentials store ~/.config/sofar/credentials.json 0600 keyed by api_url, per-clone sync-state cursor store
HTTP core: authed fetch, typed ApiError from {error:{code,message}}, retry/backoff honoring Retry-After (429/5xx/network only)
sofar login: RFC-8628 device flow (poll, slow_down, denied/expired), mint sfr_ token via POST /v1/tokens (--scopes read supported), store credentials, never print token
sofar link --org [--name]: POST /v1/repos idempotent, persist committable .sofar/remote.json; honest 404 copy (not-a-member indistinguishable from no-such-org)

Push: ulid order from genesis on first push, batch split ≤1000 lines AND ≤5MB, partial acceptance + invalid[] surfaced loudly non-fatal, idempotent re-push on 429/5xx/network, per-batch ack cursor persistence (offline queue = log after cursor)
Pull: since-cursor paging with X-Sofar-Cursor, dedupe-by-id import + projection regen, inbound cursor separate from push ack
Doorbell SSE client: data events + heartbeat comments, watchdog + reconnect with backoff, since-cursor pull on every ring and reconnect; sofar pull --watch
CLI wiring: login/link/push/pull commands, styled confirmations per CLI UI conventions (✓/└, spinners stderr-only animate-gated)
@alignlabs/sofar/client subpath export: src/lib/client.ts entry, build.mjs bundle, self-contained d.ts, side-effect-free import
docs/SPEC.md §Sync client section + invariant wording amendment + README sync quickstart
Contract tests vs in-process mock server: device flow, queue, batching/splitting, retry/backoff, cursor logic, 401/404/413/429, idempotent re-push, round-trip, downtime drill
Live E2E behind env flag (SOFAR_LIVE_API): login via manual claim+approve path, round-trip against locally running api.sofar.sh
Version 0.9.0 staged: bump, changelog/README, full suite green, user publishes (OTP)

payload.chose: Build the v2 sync CLIENT inside packages/engine as src/client/ + the @alignlabs/sofar/client subpath, under the user's explicit Phase 5.1 task (the D14 seam; server = private sofar-cloud repo, its Phases 1-4 shipped)
payload.over: Treating the CLAUDE.md Fable-window guard-rail ("DO NOT build the sync service/backend") as blocking, or a separate packages/sync workspace
payload.because: The guard-rail bans the service/backend, not the client of an already-shipped server; the task brief explicitly authorizes the client and mandates a subpath export of @alignlabs/sofar (Tauri shell + iOS consume it later), matching the existing /schema and /engine lib-surface laws (side-effect-free import, zero-runtime-deps manifest unchanged)

payload.chose: Storage split: committable .sofar/remote.json ({version, api_url, org, name, repo_id} — repo_id is not a secret, teammates share the binding); credentials in ~/.config/sofar/credentials.json (XDG_CONFIG_HOME-aware, 0600, keyed by normalized api_url); sync cursors per-clone in ~/.local/state/sofar/sync/<sha256(root)>.json (XDG_STATE_HOME-aware), invalidated when api_url/repo_id change
payload.over: Cursors in a committed .sofar file, in .git/ (absent under --root without git), or keyed by repo_id alone (two clones of one repo would share per-clone cursors)
payload.because: Ack/pull cursors mutate on every sync — committing them dirties the repo per push and invites non-union JSON merge conflicts; they are per-clone facts about what THIS checkout exchanged with the server, while the org/repo binding is team-shared truth and belongs in the record dir; idempotent push/pull by event id makes a lost cursor safe (worst case re-push/re-pull, never loss)

payload.chose: Zero new dependencies: native fetch (Node >=18) for all HTTP, a hand-rolled minimal SSE reader for the doorbell, injectable fetch/sleep for tests
payload.over: Adding an HTTP client, eventsource, or retry library
payload.because: CLAUDE.md dependency law (nothing beyond the four without a Decision) and the zero-runtime-deps manifest; the wire contract is small (JSON + NDJSON + one SSE endpoint with data/heartbeat lines) and the doorbell is notification-only, so a missed/malformed frame can never lose data — pull is the truth path

payload.chose: Amend SPEC §Architectural invariants: the zero-model-API-calls invariant stays absolute, and its "no user content sent anywhere" clause is scoped — the v2 sync client pushes record events to the user's OWN authenticated sofar-cloud repo, opt-in via sofar login + sofar link, and nothing else ever leaves the machine; a new SPEC §Sync client section carries the full client contract
payload.over: Leaving the invariant text as-is (sync would literally violate it) or weakening the invariant to allow arbitrary egress
payload.because: SPEC is authoritative over code — shipping sync without amending it would make the record lie; the invariant's intent (felt-cost D3: no model calls, no API keys, no inference costs) is untouched, and sync egress is fenced to explicit, revocable, user-owned destinations

payload.text: Server gap found during live E2E (belongs to sofar-cloud, not this repo): the /v1/doorbell SSE dies before it ever speaks — Bun.serve's default 10s idleTimeout kills the connection before the first 25s heartbeat, and response headers only flush on the first body write, so clients never even see the 200. Its own integration test passes only because it sets SOFAR_SSE_HEARTBEAT_MS=100. Fix over there: pass idleTimeout: 0 (or >heartbeat) to Bun.serve in apps/api/src/index.ts and write an initial `: connected` comment in the doorbell handler so headers flush immediately. Client-side mitigation shipped here (doorbell onGap → since-cursor pull per failed cycle), so pull --watch degrades to capped-backoff polling and loses nothing meanwhile.

payload.cmd: npm run typecheck 2>&1 | tail -3 && npm test 2>&1 | tail -5

git add -A packages/engine docs/SPEC.md && git commit -q -m "5.1+5.2: doorbell gap-degradation + live E2E against local api.sofar.sh

Every failed/dropped doorbell cycle now fires the catch-up pull (onGap),
so pull --watch degrades to capped-backoff polling when the SSE channel
is unusable — which today it is: server gap found live (Bun.serve 10s
idleTimeout kills the stream before the 25s heartbeat; headers only
flush on first write — fix tracked for sofar-cloud). Livefire suite
(SOFAR_LIVE_API-gated) proves sign-up→org, device login via the manual
claim+approve path, link, push→fresh-clone pull with zero-diff status,
idempotent re-push, and push-reaches-watcher. 717 green, livefire 4/4
against bun dev." && git show --stat HEAD e81a101 --oneline | head -20 && ls CHANGELOG.md 2>/dev/null || echo "no changelog"
